edge_hypothesis: T3 (Tillson Triple Exponential Moving Average) has never been tested as a primary baseline in any strategy. T3 applies triple EMA smoothing with a volume factor (0.7), producing significantly less lag than standard EMA while maintaining smoothness. This reduced lag should be particularly advantageous during TRANSITIONAL regimes — where the market is shifting direction and standard EMA lags behind the actual turn. Stochastic provides pullback-in-trend entry timing: when STOCHk crosses above 30, price has pulled back to oversold within the T3-confirmed trend and is beginning to recover. This is a DIFFERENT mechanism from stochastic_reversal (which uses K/D crossover in extreme zones) — here Stochastic is used purely as a threshold recovery signal (crossing above 30 = leaving oversold), not a K/D cross. The key experiment: does T3's reduced lag outperform EMA/KAMA as a baseline? If T3 produces higher transitional PF than EMA-based strategies, it validates that lag reduction is the key variable, not adaptivity (KAMA) or Gaussian smoothing (ALMA). Invalidation: (1) if trades < 30, the Stochastic 30 threshold is too restrictive — try 25; (2) if PF < RSI pullback (1.442), T3 provides no baseline advantage; (3) if transitional PF is similar to EMA-based, lag reduction is not the differentiator.

variants.name: t3_stoch_pullback_recovery_8to1
variants.description: T3 triple-smoothed trend baseline + Stochastic oversold recovery on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R. T3_10_0.7 as trend direction (close > T3 = uptrend). STOCHk crosses above 30 = price recovering from oversold pullback within the T3 trend. First-ever test of T3 as primary baseline. The low-lag hypothesis: T3 should detect trend changes faster than EMA_21, producing better transitional regime performance.
entry_long: close > T3_10_0.7 (price above T3 triple-smoothed moving average — uptrend confirmed with minimal lag).
STOCHk_14_3_3 crosses_above 30 (Stochastic K leaving oversold territory — pullback recovery timing signal).
entry_short: close < T3_10_0.7 (price below T3 triple-smoothed moving average — downtrend confirmed with minimal lag).
STOCHk_14_3_3 crosses_below 70 (Stochastic K leaving overbought territory — rally exhaustion timing signal).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, T3, KAMA, ALMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels, QQE, Choppiness Index, Vortex, STC, Stiffness.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (8.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11% win rate to break even after fees.
First test of T3 as primary baseline — measures lag reduction edge.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: T3_10_0.7 — triple-smoothed EMA with 0.7 volume factor, first-ever test as primary baseline, low-lag hypothesis
STOCHk_14_3_3 crosses_above 30 — oversold recovery timing, not K/D cross (different mechanism from standard stochastic_reversal)
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 8.0x TP — proven 8:1 sweet spot

variants.name: t3_stoch_pullback_recovery_10to1
variants.description: T3 + Stochastic on ETH/4h with 10:1 R:R. Extension test — if T3's low lag captures trend shifts earlier, the resulting trades may have more room to run. Stochastic pullback recovery within a fast-detected trend could produce larger continuations than standard EMA pullback entries. At 9% breakeven, highly forgiving.
entry_long: close > T3_10_0.7 (price above T3 triple-smoothed moving average — uptrend with minimal lag).
STOCHk_14_3_3 crosses_above 30 (Stochastic K leaving oversold — pullback recovery).
entry_short: close < T3_10_0.7 (price below T3 triple-smoothed moving average — downtrend with minimal lag).
STOCHk_14_3_3 crosses_below 70 (Stochastic K leaving overbought — rally exhaustion).

Tests whether T3 low-lag entries sustain larger R:R than EMA-based.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.0x ATR_14 from entry.
